item.title: Create your own Pokemon paradise with the latest in Nintendo life-sim
item.description: After an hour rebuilding my own deserted island back from the brink, it's safe to say our favourite pocket monsters remain irresistibly charming.

item.title: New God of War game headlines 'epic' PlayStation State of Play event
item.description: A new God of War game, exciting trailers, and several major reveals — PlayStation's February State of Play was one of the strongest showcases in recent memory.

item.title: Samsung's new phone feature blocks prying eyes
item.description: Samsung's flagship smartphone for 2026 will launch with an industry-first privacy feature aimed at stopping those prying eyes around you from glimpsing what you're looking at on your screen.

item.title: Glue Store to close in shake-up by retail giant
item.description: The company said it now wants to focus on its global brands.

item.title: Qantas unveils major change to frequent flyer program as airline records bumper $1.46 billion profit
item.description: Qantas has announced major changes to its frequent flyer program after posting a bumper $1.46 billion half-year profit.

item.title: Apple unveils $899 MacBook Neo in surprise launch
item.description: It's a brand new model for the tech giant, and it's a big shift away from the typical price point.

item.title: Former Liberal MP Rory Amon found not guilty on most child sex charges, jury deadlocked on two counts
item.description: A former MP accused of sexually assaulting a teen in a car park toilet has been left in limbo after a jury could not reach a verdict on all charges.

item.title: Apple's latest iPhone is designed for those clinging onto older phones
item.description: A lot of people using older model phones are contemplating an upgrade.

item.title: Bigfoot, trombone, orca among the latest emojis
item.description: Have you ever wanted to send a text about orcas or Bigfoot or trombones only to find you have to do it using words instead of pictures?

item.title: Major superannuation changes to become law, with a push for further tax reform later this year
item.description: The federal government's proposed changes to the way superannuation is taxed will soon become law.

item.title: Apple MacBook Neo review: The laptop market is redefined
item.description: That's AUD, not USD. How does the laptop market survive in its current form when the MacBook Neo exists?

item.title: Peter Malinauskas is the favourite going into South Australia's state election but the win might not be that s
item.description: While the result may look predictable on paper, the dynamics shaping this election are anything but simple,

item.title: Aussies heading to the USA set for high-speed Wi-Fi in a travel revolution
item.description: Australians heading to the United States are set for a big change in the way we travel, with an all-new in-flight experience on the next-generation of aircraft from this airline.

item.title: All households to suffer if 'central' industry buckles under soaring fuel costs
item.description: The global fuel crisis has left one of the nation's most essential sectors heavily exposed.

item.title: Mining giant handed $2 billion in taxpayer funds to keep Australia's second-largest aluminium smelter running
item.description: Rio Tinto has secured a $2 billion taxpayer handout to keep Australia's second-largest aluminium smelter running in a bid to save 3000 jobs.

item.title: Sydney Opera House sails light up to mark 50 Years of Apple
item.description: The sails of the Sydney Opera House will light up with colour over the next three nights as part of a global celebration of Apple's 50th birthday.

item.title: 'Almost everything has gone up': Payday super laws are coming at a fraught time for small business owners
item.description: Rebecca Foley, who co-owns Sprout Artisan Bakery in Brisbane, said ﻿unpredictable costs and new compliances have been creeping into her profit margin for years.

item.title: Kyle Sandilands addresses media after exiting court in $100 million showdown with ARN
item.description: The top-rating provocateur is suing his former employer ARN in the Federal Court.

item.title: Martina turned a childhood pastime into a seven-figure career
item.description: It was all thanks to a little nudge from her mother on an international family trip when she was eight. ﻿

item.title: Businesses urge government to decrease $3 billion tax to avoid 'even worse outcomes' in fuel crisis
item.description: "That will help control the costs in the supply chain."

item.title: Apple almost went broke – now it's celebrating 50 years
item.description: H﻿ow the company came back from the brink to become a global juggernaut.

item.title: Australia to phase out discounted junior wages for young workers
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers said the landmark ruling was a "great outcome" for workers across key sectors.

item.title: Australian cosmetics giant Mecca handed $594,000 fine for failing to lodge audited financial reports on time
item.description: Mecca's parent company and associated businesses have each coughed up ﻿$198,000 in fines after being accused of late lodgements by the corporate watchdog.

item.title: RBA to end card surcharges as cash usage dwindles
item.description: T﻿he Reserve Bank said the fees were no longer influencing people to make more efficient payments.

item.description: Inside the hidden cost of RBA's decision to scrap card surcharges.

item.description: The store will continue trading until October 2026.

item.description: "The more I looked at it, the more I discovered red flags."

item.description: T﻿heir bottom line will be impacted by any prolonged disruption of aviation fuel, analysts warn.

item.description: T﻿he CEO admitted "the roles people play are shifting".

item.description: Virgin Australia has joined Qantas in cutting flights and hiking ticket prices as skyrocketing fuel prices hit local airlines.

item.description: F﻿ollowing years of rumours, Apple may be about to deliver a missing link in its iPhone range.

item.description: A﻿ nation-wide study of our wastewater and online search data has revealed the worrying trend is booming.

item.description: Cook, who took over from the late Steve Jobs in 2011, will remain involved with Apple.

item.description: The government is set to slash the cost of running the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) and aged care under sweeping new changes.

item.description: Many critics have sounded the alarm on joining two of Hollywood's biggest remaining five studios.

item.description: Woolworths has seen a jump in grocery sales but doesn't expect it to last, slashing its profit forecasts amid high petrol prices.

item.description: The $20,000 threshold will apply per asset and will expand across tools, technology or other assets, both new and second-hand, and can be claimed in full at the end of every financial year.﻿

item.description: T﻿he One Nation leader made the remark just seconds after her adviser said 'shut up' to a journalist.

item.description: A man has been arrested in Melbourne after an alleged threat of "an incident taking place" on busy Bourke Street tonight.

item.description: P﻿olls have found that most US citizens believe aliens exist, and the latest batch of files from the Pentagon has done little to dispel the theories.

item.title: Trump posts AI video of himself attacking Stephen Colbert
item.description: Donald Trump has posted AI-generated video of himself throwing Stephen Colbert into a dumpster.

item.title: Congo curtails funeral wakes in Ebola outbreak as WHO upgrades risk assessment
item.description: Authorities in north eastern Congo banned funeral wakes and gatherings of more than 50 people on Friday.

item.title: One Nation would form federal opposition, Labor re-elected, according to new polling
item.description: Federal Labor would be returned to power, the Coalition wiped from the map in four states and One Nation the official opposition party.

item.title: At least 90 killed in China coal mine gas explosion, dozens trapped
item.description: A gas explosion at a coal mine in China’s northern province of Shanxi killed at least 82 people, state media reported Saturday and after dozens were said to be trapped underground.

item.description: "This thing is going to fail, and we don't know when."

item.description: W﻿elcome to our rolling coverage on everything federal politics on Thursday, May 28. Treasurer Jim Chalmers...

item.title: Man who attacked Lauren Huxley denied parole
item.description: Lauren's sister has described her "relief" over the fate of the attacker behind one of the most horrific crimes in modern Australian history.

item.title: Police left disappointed as three-day search for Gus fails to uncover new evidence
item.description: Police have been working on getting in contact with more than 500 people who were identified around the time of Gus' disappearance.

item.title: Iran says draft deal with US would reopen Hormuz shipping, end naval blockade
item.description: Iran's state TV says it has obtained a draft of an initial, unofficial framework for a memorandum of understanding with the US on ending their conflict.

item.title: Quarantine extended for Australian passengers of hantavirus cruise ship
item.description: The group will remain in quarantine at the Bullsbrook Centre for National Resilience, located north of Perth, for another 21 days.

item.title: Five villagers missing in flooded Laos cave for more than a week found alive, rescuers say
item.description: A video posted by a Thai rescue group involved in the mission appeared to show the moment divers emerged from the water and discovered the trapped villagers.

item.title: 'Break the law, lose the bike': Public patience with e-bikes has run out
item.description: The crackdown e-bike riders have been warned about is coming after a surge in crashes and tragic deaths.

item.title: Melbourne woman charged with terrorism offences after returning from Syria
item.description: The 34-year-old woman, ﻿who returned from Syria in September 2025, was arrested at a home in Broadmeadows in Melbourne's north today and has been charged with entering and remaining in a declared conflict zone and joining ISIS.

item.title: Accused childcare paedophile Joshua Dale Brown pleads not guilty to over 150 charges
item.description: Childcare worker Joshua Dale Brown has pleaded not guilty to abusing young children in his care and creating child abuse material.

item.title: Notorious paedophile Ashley Paul Griffith fights 'excessive' life sentence
item.description: One of Australia's worst paedophiles, childcare worker Ashley Paul Griffith, has launched a bid to slash the life sentence he received for abusing dozens of young children.

item.title: Coconut Cartel crippled, alleged on-shore leader arrested in series of raids
item.description: The Coconut Cartel is allegedly behind a spate of shootings, kidnappings, and attempted murders in a months-long gang feud.

item.title: NSW woman's last-minute swap leads to $60 million Powerball windfall
item.description: A﻿ New South Wales woman's last-minute change to her Powerball numbers has lead to a windfall totalling more than $60 million.

item.title: US and Iranian negotiators reach tentative deal to extend ceasefire and launch nuclear talks
item.description: US and Iranian negotiators reached a tentative agreement to extend the ceasefire in the three-month-old war by 60 days and launch talks on Iran's nuclear program.

item.title: Alleged victim speaks after Queensland man accused of murderous rampage
item.description: The alleged rampage took place yesterday in the small town of Childers, leaving an elderly man dead after he was allegedly brutally bashed with a metal pole.

item.title: Today in History - May 29: Queen sets herself as bait to find would-be assassin
item.description: Queen Victoria was unharmed during the second assassination attempt on her life on May 29, 1842.

item.title: Call for government to go further after bombshell $2 billion PFAS lawsuit against 3M
item.description: Concerned families in Perth's northern suburb of Bullsbrook, where the water was contaminated for years, say the health impacts will be felt for generations to come.

item.title: 'Curious' former police officer sought gigabytes of child abuse material
item.description: His fascination led him to a messaging app, where he accessed sexual imagery and videos of children.

item.title: Man jailed for 15 years over plot to attack Taylor Swift concert in Austria
item.description: A﻿n Austrian court sentenced the 21-year-old man to 15 years in jail over the terror attack plan.

item.title: Spy boss warns MPs about talking in cars
item.description: C﻿onfidential matters should only be discussed in secure locations, the ASIO deputy director-general said.

item.title: US government pushes to put Donald Trump's face on new $250 bill
item.description: The US government is working on a $250 note to bear the face of Donald Trump, his treasury secretary has announced.

item.title: 'This is serious': WHO boss's plea to locals, rebels over Ebola outbreak
item.description: D﻿irector-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus is visiting the disaster-hit country where hundreds have so far died.

item.title: ACCC takes Amazon to court over children's backpacks
item.description: I﻿t's a first-of-its-kind case involving an online marketplace, the consumer watchdog said.

item.title: Officeworks to offshore jobs to India and Philippines in $15 million restructure
item.description: The retailer reportedly axed dozens of call centre workers this week as part of its "cost-saving" transition.

item.title: Musicians back out of Trump's Washington concert after widespread criticism
item.description: A series of musicians announced for a concert organised by the Trump administration have pulled out a day after it was announced.

item.title: Son locked his dementia-suffering father in blacked-out room to steal his fortune
item.description: An elderly New Zealand man with advanced dementia was subjected to what his son later admitted was "torture" inside a locked, blacked-out room while family members systematically stole his life savings.

item.title: KPMG chief executive resigns over firm's management of whistleblower allegations
item.description: T﻿he management of a whistleblower's allegations has ultimately been found to be insufficient.

item.title: Blue Origin New Glenn rocket explodes into fireball during test launch in Florida
item.description: Footage from NASA's live stream shows the ﻿Blue Origin New Glenn rocket bursting into flames on the pad about 9pm local time.

item.title: Millions of middle-aged Aussie men drinking at risky levels, new study finds
item.description: R﻿isky drinking is still prevalent in this demographic, and researchers are looking into why that's so.

item.title: The free ride for Victorian commuters officially ends soon. Here's what you need to know
item.description: Commuters will soon pay for buses, trains and trams after a long period of fare-free travel.

item.title: 'One or two notches tighter': NAB's decision to hike fixed rates spells doom for borrowers
item.description: NAB today raised its short-term fixed rate for loans by 0.15 percentage points - the first of the major banks to do so.

item.title: Iran recovering buried missile arsenal
item.description: A "mega rocket" owned by the fourth richest person in the world has met a fiery end.

item.title: What is H3N2? The new flu strain set to smash Aussies this winter
item.description: I﻿t is associated with severe seasonal epidemics, high hospitalisations and deaths.

item.title: Joyce joins former colleague in call for snap election
item.description: H﻿ello and welcome to our rolling coverage on everything federal politics on Friday, May 29.Opposition Lead...

item.title: Mushroom killer Erin Patterson's appeal against triple murder conviction set for August
item.description: T﻿he triple murderer's appeal will be heard in Victoria's Supreme Court over two days on August 19-20.

item.title: Rents to jump 350 per cent under budget measures, modelling shows
item.description: R﻿esearch commissioned by property bodies disputes the federal government's budget claims.

item.title: Suspected sovereign citizen arrested after ute rams into police station during decorated cop's send-off
item.description: The crash happened just as a march was about to take place for Detective ﻿Senior Constable Scott Whale, who is retiring after 40 years in the NSW Police Force.

item.title: Former rugby league player Garry Sullivan extradited to Melbourne over 1994 armed robbery and shooting
item.description: Garry Sullivan, 78, was arrested yesterday after police executed search warrants at two Gold Coast addresse.

item.title: Drone carrying drugs, phone intercepted at Queensland prison
item.description: A drone carrying more than $1 million of contraband into Townsville Correctional Complex in North Queensland has been intercepted by ﻿authorities.

item.title: Protesters arrested at new Olympic stadium site Victoria Park
item.description: Police have arrested two people at Victoria Park, after council officers approached First Nations protesters who have been camping out in opposition to the construction of the 63,000 seat Brisbane Stadium, the centrepiece of the Brisbane 2032 Olympic Games.

item.title: Promising new MND drug shown to reduce key disease markers
item.description: P﻿romising data has led the Therapeutic Goods Administration to grant provisional approval of the drug, giving eligible patients in Australia faster access.

item.title: Aussie state bracing for 'once-in-five-year' storm bringing 'risk of tornadoes'
item.description: Huge swells, destructive winds and heavy rain will accompany "strongest winter system we see this year".
